metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Highly competitive Agreeableness (17th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Moderate Conscientiousness (53rd pct)
Highly introverted Extraversion (7th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Stable Neuroticism (30th pct) — Emotionally stable, calm, resilient
Highly open Openness to Experience (85th pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (18th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Moderate Achievement Striving (50th pct)
  Moderate Self-Discipline (55th pct)
  Very Low Activity Level (5th pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (0th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (5th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  Low Anger / Hostility (37th pct) — Slow to anger, easy-going
  Low Self-Consciousness (24th pct) — Comfortable socially, not easily embarrassed
  Stable Neuroticism (32nd pct) — Emotionally stable, calm, resilient
  Moderate Chronological Age (42nd pct)

message.reasoning:
We need to simulate a baseline opinion on "Trust In Mainstream Journalism" for this profile. The profile is a 42-year-old, sex "Other", with very low extraversion (7th percentile), very high openness (85th), low agreeableness (17th), moderate conscientiousness (53rd), and low neuroticism (30th). Specific facets: very low trust (18.9th percentile), very low compliance (9.7th), very low modesty (10.6th), low straightforwardness (23rd), low altruism (20.5th), low tender-mindedness (17.6th). High openness to ideas (93.5th), values (88.5th), fantasy (94.7th), actions (93.6th). Low positive emotions (5th), low activity level (5th), low assertiveness (0th), low excitement seeking (0th). Moderate conscientiousness facets.

Interpretation: This person is highly open, introverted, emotionally stable, and disagreeable (low agreeableness). Low trust suggests skepticism towards institutions and people. Low compliance suggests they don't easily conform to authority or accepted norms. Low tender-mindedness suggests they are tough-minded, objective, unsympathetic. High openness to ideas and values suggests they are intellectually curious, likely to question conventional wisdom, and may have non-traditional beliefs. Low extraversion and low positive emotions suggest they are serious, reserved, and not easily swayed by social desirability.

Given the policy item is "Trust In Mainstream Journalism", this profile likely has low trust in mainstream journalism. Low trust facet, low agreeableness, high openness to ideas and values all point to skepticism. However, they are not extremely high in neuroticism (low anxiety, low anger), so it's not a paranoid distrust but more a calm, critical skepticism. They are also moderate in conscientiousness, so they might still engage with information but critically. The score should be negative, moderately strong but not extreme. I'd estimate around -600 to -700, but avoid round numbers. Given distribution guide: ~20% strongly leaning (600-850), this fits. But also note the low extraversion and low positive emotions might mean they are not actively campaigning against, but firm disagreement. I'll choose -672.

Confidence: moderate, 0.72. Reasoning: combine low trust facet, low agreeableness, high openness.
